title: Hodgkin s Lymphoma
authors: Caron A. Jacobson, Dan L. Longo

items.title: Epidemiology of Hodgkin's Lymphoma (HL)
items.content: Hodgkin's lymphoma is a malignancy originating from mature B lymphocytes, accounting for approximately 10% of all lymphomas diagnosed annually. It has a stable incidence with an estimated 8830 new cases in the United States in 2023. HL shows a bimodal age distribution, peaking in individuals in their twenties and again in those in their eighties.
keyPoints: HL represents ~10% of all lymphomas diagnosed each year.
Estimated 8830 new cases in the U.S. in 2023.
Bimodal age distribution: peaks in twenties and eighties.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.title: Risk Factors and Subtypes of Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma (cHL)
items.content: Classical HL is more common in whites, males, and those with HIV infection. It has four histopathologic subtypes: nodular sclerosis, mixed cellularity, lymphocyte-rich, and lymphocyte-depleted. Nodular sclerosis is prevalent among younger patients in the U.S., while mixed-cellularity or lymphocyte-depleted types are more common in elderly, HIV-infected individuals, and those in developing countries.
keyPoints: More common in whites than blacks and males than females.
Four subtypes: nodular sclerosis, mixed cellularity, lymphocyte-rich, lymphocyte-depleted.
Nodular sclerosis is prevalent among younger U.S. patients; mixed-cellularity or lymphocyte-depleted types more common in elderly and HIV-infected individuals.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.title: Role of Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV) in Hodgkin's Lymphoma
items.content: There is an association between EBV and HL, with 20-40% of HL patients showing monoclonal or oligoclonal proliferation of EBV-infected cells. In HIV-associated cHL, nearly all cases have detectable EBV, suggesting a significant role in lymphomagenesis through the expression of latent membrane protein 1 (LMP-1) by Reed-Sternberg cells.
keyPoints: 20–40% of HL patients show monoclonal or oligoclonal proliferation of EBV-infected cells.
Nearly all HIV-associated cHL cases have detectable EBV.
EBV may play a role in lymphomagenesis via LMP-1 expression by Reed-Sternberg cells.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.title: Presentation and Initial Evaluation of Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma
items.content: Patients with classical Hodgkin's lymphoma (cHL) typically present with palpable, nontender lymphadenopathy in the neck, supraclavicular area, and axilla. More than half have mediastinal adenopathy at diagnosis. Initial evaluation includes history for 'B' symptoms (fevers, night sweats, weight loss), physical examination of lymph nodes, liver, and spleen size, and laboratory tests such as CBC with differential, ESR, chemistry studies, and HIV/hepatitis testing.
keyPoints: Palpable nontender lymphadenopathy in neck, supraclavicular area, axilla
>50% have mediastinal adenopathy at diagnosis
'B' symptoms: fevers, night sweats, weight loss
Physical examination includes liver and spleen size assessment
Laboratory evaluation: CBC with differential, ESR, chemistry studies, HIV/hepatitis testing
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma

items.title: Staging and Prognostic Factors in Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma
items.content: Staging of cHL is anatomically based using the Ann Arbor system. Early-stage disease (stage I or II) has a better prognosis, further classified by factors like bulky disease, number of lymph node areas involved, ESR (>30 with 'B' symptoms; >50 without), and age. Advanced-stage disease prognosis uses the International Prognostic Score (IPS), which considers male sex, age >45, stage IV disease, serum albumin <4 g/dL, hemoglobin <10.5 g/dL, WBC count ≥15,000/μL, and lymphocyte count <600/μL or <8% of WBC.
keyPoints: Staging uses Ann Arbor system
Early-stage disease: stage I or II
Prognostic factors for early-stage: bulky disease, number of lymph node areas involved, ESR (>30 with 'B' symptoms; >50 without), age
Advanced-stage prognosis uses IPS
IPS factors: male sex, age >45, stage IV, serum albumin <4 g/dL, hemoglobin <10.5 g/dL, WBC ≥15,000/μL, lymphocyte count <600/μL or <8% of WBC
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma

items.title: Diagnostic Approach and Differential Diagnosis for Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma
items.content: Diagnosis of cHL is confirmed by biopsy reviewed by an expert hematopathologist, identifying rare neoplastic B-cells in a polyclonal inflammatory infiltrate. PET/CT scan is preferred over bone marrow biopsy for staging due to patchy involvement. Differential diagnosis includes inflammatory processes, mononucleosis, non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL), phenytoin-induced adenopathy, and nonlymphomatous malignancies.
keyPoints: Diagnosis confirmed by expert-reviewed biopsy
Rare neoplastic B-cells in polyclonal inflammatory infiltrate
PET/CT preferred for staging over bone marrow biopsy
Differential diagnosis: inflammatory processes, mononucleosis, NHL, phenytoin-induced adenopathy, nonlymphomatous malignancies
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma

items.title: Optimal Treatment Strategy for Early-Stage HL
items.content: In early-stage Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma, the use of ABVD chemotherapy alone without radiation therapy is effective in patients with low-risk disease. This approach results in high progression-free and overall survival rates while avoiding late toxicities associated with radiation.
keyPoints: ABVD regimen (doxorubicin, bleomycin, vinblastine, dacarbazine) every other week
4-6 cycles of ABVD alone for low-risk disease results in 88–92% progression-free and 97–100% overall survival at 5–7 years
Slightly increased risk of relapse compared to combined-modality therapy with radiation, but no difference in overall survival due to effective salvage strategies
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EARLY-STAGE DISEASE

items.title: Role of PET/CT Scans in Treatment Decisions for Early-Stage HL
items.content: Early interim PET/CT scans can guide the duration and extent of therapy in early-stage Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma. A negative PET/CT scan after a few cycles of ABVD may allow omission of further radiation therapy.
keyPoints: Negative PET/CT scan after 3 cycles of ABVD predicts excellent outcomes with no additional therapy
Negative PET/CT scan after 2 cycles of ABVD predicts good outcomes with two more cycles of ABVD alone, without radiation
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EARLY-STAGE DISEASE

items.title: Treatment Considerations for Unfavorable-Risk Early-Stage HL
items.content: In unfavorable-risk early-stage Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma, omitting radiation therapy after chemotherapy increases relapse risk but does not affect overall survival. Treatment decisions depend on disease extent and contraindications to radiation.
keyPoints: Omission of radiation post-chemotherapy in unfavorable-risk disease leads to increased relapse risk
No change in overall survival despite increased relapse risk due to effective salvage strategies
Treatment options: ABVD for 4 cycles followed by involved field radiation or ABVD alone for 6 cycles
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EARLY-STAGE DISEASE

items.title: Chemotherapy Alone vs. Chemotherapy with Brentuximab in Advanced-Stage Hodgkin Lymphoma
items.content: For patients with advanced-stage Hodgkin lymphoma, the addition of radiation therapy after a complete response to chemotherapy alone does not confer additional benefits and should be avoided. Instead, treatment regimens such as ABVD or brentuximab vedotin combined with doxorubicin, vinblastine, and dacarbazine (AVD) are recommended for six cycles. The ESCHELON-1 study demonstrated a progression-free and overall survival benefit when using brentuximab-AVD over ABVD.
keyPoints: Radiation therapy does not improve outcomes after complete response to chemotherapy alone in advanced-stage disease.
Common regimens include ABVD or brentuximab vedotin plus AVD for six cycles.
Brentuximab targets CD30 on HRS cells and is conjugated with MMAE.
ESCHELON-1 study showed progression-free and overall survival benefits with brentuximab-AVD over ABVD.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · ADVANCED-STAGE DISEASE

items.title: Role of PD-1/PD-L1 Inhibitors in Relapsed Hodgkin Lymphoma
items.content: In the setting of relapsed Hodgkin lymphoma, drugs targeting the PD-1/PD-L1 axis, such as pembrolizumab and nivolumab, have shown high response rates with durable effects. These agents are particularly effective due to genomic alterations leading to PD-L1 overexpression on HRS cells. Phase 2 studies combining these inhibitors with chemotherapy regimens like brentuximab or ICE/GND have achieved high complete response rates, facilitating autologous stem cell transplantation.
keyPoints: PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors (e.g., pembrolizumab, nivolumab) are effective in relapsed Hodgkin lymphoma due to PD-L1 overexpression on HRS cells.
High response rates and durable responses have been observed with these drugs.
Combination therapies with brentuximab or ICE/GND chemotherapy regimens achieve high complete response rates, enabling autologous stem cell transplantation.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · ADVANCED-STAGE DISEASE

items.title: Salvage Therapy for Relapsed Hodgkin Lymphoma
items.content: Patients relapsing after primary therapy of HL often can still be cured. Standard salvage chemotherapy regimens, such as ICE and GND, are used to document sensitivity and reduce tumor mass. However, newer combinations like brentuximab with immune checkpoint inhibitors (nivolumab or pembrolizumab) have shown higher efficacy and durable response rates post-autologous stem cell transplantation.
keyPoints: Standard salvage chemotherapy regimens include ICE and GND.
Newer combinations involve brentuximab with nivolumab or pembrolizumab.
These newer combinations lead to high complete remission rates and success in autologous stem cell transplantation.
Chemotherapy/immunotherapy combinations are more effective than standard salvage chemotherapy.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · RELAPSED DISEASE

items.title: Role of Autologous Stem Cell Transplantation in Relapsed Hodgkin Lymphoma
items.content: For patients who respond completely or nearly so to alternative salvage chemotherapy, autologous stem cell transplantation can cure over half. Studies are ongoing to determine if transplant is necessary for those with favorable responses to chemotherapy/immunotherapy combinations. Brentuximab maintenance post-transplant has shown benefits in the AETHERA study.
keyPoints: Autologous stem cell transplantation cures over half of patients who respond well to salvage chemotherapy.
Ongoing studies investigate necessity of transplant after favorable response to chemotherapy/immunotherapy.
Brentuximab maintenance therapy post-transplant is beneficial, as shown in the AETHERA study.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · RELAPSED DISEASE

items.question: Which subtype of Hodgkin's lymphoma is most common among younger patients diagnosed in the United States?
items.explanation: The nodular sclerosing subtype of HL is largely seen in younger age groups in the United States.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.question: What percentage of Hodgkin's lymphoma cases are accounted for by nodular sclerosis and mixed-cellularity types combined?
items.explanation: Nodular sclerosis and mixed-cellularity types account for nearly 95% of Hodgkin's lymphoma cases.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.question: Which virus is detected in nearly all cases of HIV-associated classical Hodgkin's lymphoma?
items.explanation: EBV can be detected in nearly all cases of HIV-associated classical Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.question: What is the typical expression pattern of CD30 on Reed-Sternberg cells in classical Hodgkin's lymphoma?
items.explanation: Reed-Sternberg cells express CD30 in 100% of cases of classical Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.question: Which lymph node groups are most commonly involved at diagnosis in patients with classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma (cHL)?
items.explanation: Most patients with cHL present with palpable lymphadenopathy in the neck, supraclavicular area, and axilla.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma

items.question: What is Pel-Ebstein fever associated with?
options: A pattern of persistent fevers without intervals
A pattern of intermittent fevers followed by afebrile periods
items.explanation: Pel-Ebstein fever is characterized by fevers that persist for days to weeks, followed by afebrile intervals and then recurrence of the fever.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma

items.question: Which diagnostic tool is more accurate than bone marrow biopsy for evaluating bone marrow involvement in cHL?
items.explanation: A PET/CT scan is used for staging and is more accurate than a bone marrow biopsy for evaluating bone marrow involvement in cHL.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma

items.question: What characterizes the neoplastic cells of classical Hodgkin's lymphoma?
options: They are T-cell origin and express immunoglobulin genes.
They are B-cell origin with rearranged but unexpressed immunoglobulin genes.
They are polyclonal inflammatory infiltrate without any neoplastic cells.
They are derived from myeloid lineage.
items.explanation: HL is characterized by rare neoplastic cells of B-cell origin with rearranged but not expressed immunoglobulin genes.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma

items.question: What is the most common chemotherapy regimen used to treat early-stage HL in the United States?
items.explanation: The passage states that ABVD (doxorubicin, bleomycin, vinblastine, and dacarbazine) is the most common chemotherapy regimen used in the United States for early-stage HL.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EARLY-STAGE DISEASE

items.question: What are the progression-free and overall survival rates at 5–7 years with four to six cycles of ABVD alone in low-risk early-stage HL?
items.explanation: The passage mentions that four to six cycles of ABVD alone result in progression-free survival rates of 88–92% and overall survival rates of 97–100% at 5–7 years.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EARLY-STAGE DISEASE

items.question: What is the impact of a negative PET/CT scan after three cycles of ABVD on treatment decisions?
options: It indicates poor outcomes with additional therapy needed.
It predicts excellent outcomes without additional therapy.
It suggests switching to an alternative chemotherapy regimen.
It implies that radiation therapy is mandatory.
items.explanation: The passage states that a negative PET/CT scan after three cycles of ABVD predicted excellent outcomes with no additional therapy.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EARLY-STAGE DISEASE

items.question: What is the risk associated with omitting radiation therapy in patients with unfavorable-risk early-stage HL?
options: No change in overall survival but increased risk of relapse.
items.explanation: The passage notes that omitting radiation therapy in unfavorable-risk disease is associated with a more significant increased risk of relapse, but no change in overall survival.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Classical Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EARLY-STAGE DISEASE

items.question: Which chemotherapy regimen has demonstrated both progression-free and overall survival benefits in advanced-stage classical Hodgkin lymphoma (cHL) according to the ESCHELON-1 study?
items.explanation: The ESCHELON-1 study showed a benefit with brentuximab-AVD over ABVD.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · ADVANCED-STAGE DISEASE

items.question: Which treatment is not associated with an improvement in overall survival but has increased toxicity for advanced-stage Hodgkin lymphoma?
items.explanation: Both Stanford V and escalated BEACOPP are associated with increased toxicity without improving overall survival.

items.question: What is the recommended treatment for patients who achieve a complete response to chemotherapy alone in advanced-stage Hodgkin lymphoma?
options: Continue with additional cycles of chemotherapy
items.explanation: Patients who achieve a complete response to chemotherapy alone do not benefit from the addition of radiation therapy.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · ADVANCED-STAGE DISEASE

items.question: Which of the following chemotherapy regimens is NOT traditionally included as a standard salvage therapy for relapsed Hodgkin lymphoma?
items.explanation: ABVD is not listed among the traditional standard salvage chemotherapy regimens; ICE and GND are, while newer combinations include brentuximab with immune checkpoint inhibitors.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · RELAPSED DISEASE

items.question: What is a common outcome for patients who achieve complete or near-complete response to alternative salvage chemotherapy in relapsed Hodgkin lymphoma?
options: They are usually not curable with autologous stem cell transplantation
They have low chances of remission after further treatment
Autologous stem cell transplantation can cure over half of these patients
Radiation therapy is the next step for all such patients
items.explanation: For patients who respond completely or nearly so to alternative salvage chemotherapy, autologous stem cell transplantation can cure over half.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · RELAPSED DISEASE

items.question: Which therapy is used as a maintenance treatment following successful autologous stem cell transplantation in relapsed Hodgkin lymphoma?
items.explanation: Brentuximab is used as a maintenance therapy following successful autologous stem cell transplantation, based on the AETHERA study.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · RELAPSED DISEASE

items.statement: Infection by Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) is a confirmed etiologic factor in all cases of Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.explanation: The role of EBV as an etiologic factor in HL is suggested but not definitively established for all cases.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.statement: Reed-Sternberg cells are the malignant cells in Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.explanation: Reed-Sternberg (HRS) cells are indeed the malignant cells characteristic of Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · Hodgkin's Lymphoma | EPIDEMIOLOGY AND ETIOLOGY

items.statement: The Ann Arbor staging system is used for determining the anatomic stage in classical Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.explanation: Staging for cHL is done using the Ann Arbor staging system.

items.statement: Bone marrow biopsy is more accurate than PET/CT scan in evaluating bone marrow involvement in classical Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.explanation: A PET/CT scan is more accurate than a bone marrow biopsy for evaluation of bone marrow involvement.

items.statement: The use of combined-modality therapy (chemotherapy and radiation) for early-stage HL has been shown to improve long-term survival.
items.explanation: The passage indicates that the addition of radiation therapy results in minimal or no improvement in long-term survival.

items.statement: German studies have shown that a very abbreviated chemotherapy regimen followed by low-dose radiation is equally effective to standard combined-modality therapy for good-risk early-stage HL.
items.explanation: The passage mentions German studies found two cycles of ABVD and 20 Gy of radiation were as effective as four cycles of ABVD with 30 Gy of radiation for good-risk disease.

items.statement: Brentuximab is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting CD30 on the HRS cell.
items.explanation: Brentuximab targets CD30 and is used in combination with chemotherapy for advanced-stage cHL.

items.statement: Patients who relapse after an effective chemotherapy regimen are usually curable with subsequent standard-dose chemotherapy.
items.explanation: These patients are usually not curable with subsequent chemotherapy administered at standard doses.
items.reference: Hodgkin s Lymphoma · RELAPSED DISEASE

items.statement: Anti-CD30 chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy has shown promising early results in multiple relapsed cHL.
items.explanation: The passage states that anti-CD30 CAR T-cell therapy has been tested with promising early results.

items.assertion: The incidence of Hodgkin's lymphoma is higher in males than females.
items.reason: HL is more common in whites than in blacks and has a bimodal age distribution.
items.explanation: While the assertion is true, the reason provided does not explain why HL incidence is higher in males.

items.assertion: The majority of Hodgkin's lymphoma cases are classical Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.reason: Nodular sclerosis and mixed-cellularity types account for nearly 95% of HL cases.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why the majority of HL cases are classical.

items.assertion: Patients with early-stage classical Hodgkin's lymphoma have a better prognosis overall.
items.reason: Early-stage disease is classified as stage I or II, which generally has a more favorable outcome.
items.explanation: The assertion and reason are both true, and the reason correctly explains why early-stage patients have a better prognosis.

items.assertion: The International Prognostic Score (IPS) is used to predict outcomes in early-stage classical Hodgkin's lymphoma.
items.reason: IPS assigns points based on factors like male sex, older age, and low serum albumin.
items.explanation: The assertion is false because IPS is used for predicting prognosis in advanced-stage disease, not early-stage.

items.assertion: ABVD is less effective than alternative regimens like Stanford V or escalated BEACOPP for early-stage HL.

items.assertion: Newer chemotherapy/immunotherapy combinations have largely replaced standard salvage chemotherapy approaches.
items.reason: These newer combinations are associated with higher rates of durable response after autologous stem cell transplantation due to chemosensitizing effects from immune checkpoint inhibition.
items.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains why newer combinations have replaced standard approaches.
